metadata.consultant: Daniela Reyes, Senior Legal Technology Consultant

keyDecisions.title: Calendar & Deadlines downgraded from 2 to 1
keyDecisions.rationale: Confirmed missed deadline with no process change justifies the lowest maturity score.

keyDecisions.title: License Utilization upgraded from 2 to 3
keyDecisions.rationale: Core platform usage is functional — the gap is in secondary features, not fundamentals.

keyDecisions.title: Single Point of Failure — Office Manager Dependency
keyDecisions.rationale: No backup, no documentation, no cross-training for the person who runs all firm operations.

keyDecisions.title: Conflict Checking Relies on Attorney Memory
keyDecisions.rationale: Live demonstration during assessment revealed an undetected near-miss conflict.

keyDecisions.title: No Defined Communication Cadence
keyDecisions.rationale: 8 to 12 weekly status calls consuming 5 to 8 hours of staff time — a structural client experience gap.

keyDecisions.title: Voice-to-text for senior partner
keyDecisions.rationale: Preserves preferred dictation workflow while eliminating the 3 to 5 day reconstruction lag costing $60K+ annually.

keyDecisions.title: Structured billing calendar
keyDecisions.rationale: Compresses the 12-day review bottleneck to 5 days by setting deadlines attorneys have never been given.

timeline.title: API Analysis & Questionnaire Review
activities: Reviewed automated baseline findings from Clio API data
Analyzed questionnaire responses (22 sections completed by office manager)
Identified preliminary enrichment patterns across all 7 domains
Prepared interview questions based on data-questionnaire convergence
Flagged 5 findings for deeper investigation during stakeholder interviews

timeline.title: Stakeholder Interviews — Day 1
activities: 45-minute interview with Robert Brennan (Managing Partner)
30-minute interview with Maria Santos (Office Manager)
Observed live time entry, billing, and calendar workflows
Confirmed missed deadline details and manual calculation process
Demonstrated permission settings to office manager — immediate recognition of risk
Promoted 5 interview responses to finding annotations

timeline.title: Stakeholder Interviews — Day 2 & Analysis
activities: 30-minute interview with Andrea Torres (Paralegal)
Observed deadline calculation workflow using personal spreadsheet
Conducted live conflict check demonstration — identified near-miss
Created 3 custom findings based on interview observations
Wrote 6 annotations across findings 3.1, 3.3, 2.3, 7.1, 1.1, and 4.1
Drafted score override justifications for findings 2.3 and 6.2

timeline.title: Report Preparation & Delivery
activities: Finalized all annotations and score overrides
Completed custom finding documentation with observation, impact, and recommendation
Built prioritized 4-phase improvement roadmap
Prepared executive summary and domain narratives
Delivered preliminary findings to managing partner in a 30-minute closing session

interviewLog.keyTopics: Utilization variance, Karen Clark workflow, billing bottleneck, missed deadline, adoption gap, revenue impact awareness
Permission awareness, billing draft workflow, trust accounting process, single point of failure risk, client status calls
Deadline calculation process, court rules request history, calendar linking friction, document organization in Google Drive

preEngagementBriefing: Brennan & Clark LLP is a 4-attorney general practice firm in Tampa, Florida, operating on Clio Manage Essentials since 2019 (self-implemented). The firm added Clio Grow approximately 18 months ago but adoption appears partial. The Clio Efficiency Checklist score places them in the Basic Use tier (estimated 42 of 85 points), indicating functional setup with significant missed opportunities. Revenue is approximately $610,000 annually across 5 practice areas. The automated baseline reveals a 27% utilization rate (likely lower when adjusted for actual hours worked), a 38-day billing cycle with $127,000 in unbilled inventory, and an 80% admin access rate with no permission groups. The firm's questionnaire responses suggest awareness of gaps but limited bandwidth for improvement. Priority areas for investigation: the utilization variance across attorneys (8% to 41% range suggests individual workflow issues, not firm-wide problems), the billing cycle bottleneck (12-day draft aging suggests a review process problem), and the adoption gap (50x difference between most and least active users). The managing partner has expressed interest in getting more value from Clio and improving billing efficiency. Approach the engagement as a collaborative assessment, not an audit.
